topic: Someone receives a friend request from a childhood bully
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification blinked on my screen, a jarring intrusion into the quiet hum of my afternoon. My breath hitched, a small, involuntary catch in my chest. It was a profile picture I hadn't seen in fifteen years—a smug, familiar grin that used to send a cold knot of anxiety through me. I stared at the request, then slowly, deliberately, I tapped the 'Accept' button. A slow, almost imperceptible smile stretched across my lips, the kind that doesn't quite reach the eyes but settles deep in the jaw. I leaned back in my chair, the tension draining out of my shoulders, replaced by a warm, heavy sense of completion. It was done.

[story 2]

When the ping arrived, I didn't even look up from the book I was reading. My fingers paused mid-sentence, the paper momentarily forgotten. A flicker of something—not anger, but a deep, settled calm—washed over me. I read the name, and a faint, almost musical sound escaped my throat, a low chuckle that vibrated in my chest. I closed the book gently, placing it on the desk. I picked up my phone, my thumb hovering over the acceptance button for a beat, savoring the anticipation. Then, with a decisive press, I clicked. A sense of quiet triumph bloomed in my core, making my posture straighten almost unconsciously.

[story 3]

The alert popped up, bright and unwelcome. For a second, my stomach tightened, a familiar, unwelcome clench of old nerves. But then I looked at the profile picture—the one I’d spent years trying to erase from my memory. A slow, deliberate exhale escaped me, a release of air I hadn't realized I was holding. I typed a brief, neutral message, something utterly devoid of warmth, and hit send. As the screen went dark, I leaned my head against the cool surface of the desk. My muscles relaxed completely, a profound sense of having finally closed a very old, irritating chapter settling over me like a comfortable blanket.
